topic_title: For loops — repeating an action across a list

format_discipline.first_line: # For loops — repeating an action across a list
format_discipline.detail: No frontmatter. Correct 6-section structure in prescribed order. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and valid.

length_budget.detail: 1208 words at 230 wpm ≈ 5.25 min; ceiling estimate 6 min. In range [6, 8] ✓.

citation_discipline.detail: All 5 primary resources cited. [1] Python docs for-statements cited in Overview, Key Concepts table, accumulator section, Worked Example, In Practice. [2] Real Python for-loop guide cited throughout. [3] W3Schools cited in for-loop anatomy table. [4] Python docs range() cited in range() table. [5] Real Python lists cited in lists section, Overview, Key Takeaways.

forward_references.detail: enumerate() named-and-deferred in In Practice ('introduced in a later topic'). No other forward references. All code uses only concepts from 11.1–11.6 (list, for loop, range(), accumulator, if/else from 11.5, input() + str() from 11.4–11.5).

beginner_readability.detail: All terms defined on first use: list (Key Concepts), element (Key Concepts), index (Key Concepts), loop variable (Key Concepts table), range() (Key Concepts range table), accumulator (worked accumulator block with += shorthand explained). One idea per paragraph. Concrete examples before abstract pattern.

formatting.detail: For-loop anatomy presented as a 4-column table (part / example / role). range() variants presented as a 2-column table. Accumulator explained with inline comments. Worked Example has annotated steps. No walls of text — paragraphs ≤4 sentences throughout.

scope.detail: Content stays within list literals + for loop + range() + accumulator + for+if combination. enumerate() and sum() named-and-deferred. While loops, list comprehensions, nested loops not mentioned.

corpus_faithfulness.detail: All claims map to corpus §§5.1–5.5 and §6–§7. Worked Example follows corpus §6 generalised grade-average script exactly. No new claims introduced.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1). 1208-word, 6-min reading is clean: no frontmatter, correct 6-section structure, all 5 primary resources cited, no forward references, no scope-creep. Diagram embedded in Key Concepts. For-loop anatomy table and range() table are clear learning aids.
